label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out. The question is about the story "The First Hello" and asks which option correctly fills in the blank regarding fitting a working telephone booth. First, I need to recall the details from the story. The first paragraph mentions that "It was a year ago that the phone line had been installed, six months later men from the public works department had come to put up the phone booth." So, the phone line was installed a year ago, then six months later the booth was put up. That would make the total time from the installation of the line to the booth being put up as 1.5 years. But the question is about fitting a working telephone booth. The story also mentions that the booth was installed six months after the line, and then there was a painter who took a day to paint the sign. However, the telephone man at the end says that the minister will come next month to inaugurate it, implying that even though the booth is there, it's not yet officially in use. Looking at the options: (A) was a process that had already taken 12 months - The phone line
